hierarchy.title: भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक
hierarchy.text:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक

RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/DOR/2025-26/298 DOR.GOV.REC.No.217/18.10.015/2025-26 November 28, 2025
Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Governance) Directions,
2025 (Updated as on May 25, 2026)

children.title: Table of Contents
children.text: Table of Contents

hierarchy.title: Chapter II - Constitution of Board and Appointment of Chief Executive Officer (CEO) /
hierarchy.text:
Chapter II - Constitution of Board and Appointment of Chief Executive Officer (CEO) /
Managing Director (MD) ...................................................................................................... 3

hierarchy.title: Chapter III - Role of the Board and Individual Directors................................................... 5

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
In exercise of the powers conferred by Section 35A read with Section 56 of
the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 and all other provisions / laws enabling the Reserve
Bank of India ('RBI') in this regard, RBI being satisfied that it is necessary and
expedient in the public interest so to do, hereby issues the Directions hereinafter
specified.
Chapter-I Preliminary

children.title: A. Short title and commencement
children.text:
A. Short title and commencement
1.
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative
Banks - Governance) Directions, 2025.
2.
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect.

children.title: B. Applicability
children.text:
B. Applicability
3.
These Directions shall be applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks (hereinafter
collectively referred to as 'RCBs' and individually as 'RCB').
In this context, 'rural co-operative banks' shall mean State Co-operative Banks
(collectively referred to as 'StCBs' and individually as 'StCB') and Central Co-
operative Banks (collectively referred to as 'CCBs' and individually as 'CCB'), as
defined in the National Bank for Agriculture and Rural Development Act, 1981.

children.title: C. Definitions
children.text:
C. Definitions
4.
In these directions, unless the context states otherwise, the terms herein shall
bear the meanings assigned to them below:
(1) 'Board' means the Board of Directors ('BoD') or the governing body of an
RCB, by whatever name called, to which the direction and control of the
management of affairs of the RCB is entrusted.
(2) 'Director' means a director appointed on the Board of an RCB, by way of
election or co-option or in any other manner as per the applicable laws.
5.
All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, or any statutory modification or re-enactment
thereto, or Glossary of Terms published by RBI or as used in common or
commercial parlance, as the case may be.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-II Constitution of Board and Appointment of Chief Executive Officer
(CEO) / Managing Director (MD)
6.
The directors of an RCB should be knowledgeable and persons of high integrity.
They must function in a cohesive manner and provide leadership for the smooth
and efficient management of the affairs of the RCB.
7.
The following persons shall not be eligible to become directors of an RCB:
(1) those who are not eligible for admission even as members (except co-opted
and nominated directors);
(2) those engaged in money lending, financing and investment activities, either
in individual capacity or as proprietor / partner / employee / director of any
concern; and
(3) those convicted of any criminal offense involving moral turpitude.
17A. A director on the Board of an RCB, after completing a continuous tenure of ten
years in office, shall be eligible to be re-appointed, whether by election or co-
option or in any other manner, as a director on the Board of the same RCB only
after undergoing a minimum cooling-off period of three years. During the cooling-
off period, the said director shall not be associated with the RCB in any capacity
/ manner other than as a member / customer. This, however, shall not preclude
him / her from being appointed as a director on the Board of another bank, if
otherwise eligible.

children.title: Explanation:
children.text:
Explanation:
For calculating the period of continuous tenure, the total time served on the Board
of the RCB including the period of directorship preceding an interruption of less
than three years but excluding the period of directorship preceding at least a
three-year interruption shall be reckoned.
8.
To ensure professionalism in the Board, an RCB shall have at least two directors
with suitable banking experience (at middle / senior management level) or with
relevant professional qualifications i.e., in areas of accountancy / banking /
1 Inserted with effect from May 25, 2026 vide Reserve Bank of India (Rural Co-operative Banks - Governance) Amendment Directions, 2026 dated May 25, 2026.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
agriculture and development / legal / auditing. An RCB should also have a
suitable provision in its byelaws to ensure such professionals on its Board.
9.
The appointment, reappointment, and termination of appointment of a Chief
Executive Officer (CEO) / Managing Director (MD) shall, in terms of Section
35B(1)(b) read with Section 56 of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, require the
prior approval of RBI. Applications in this regard shall be submitted to RBI
through the PRAVAAH portal (https://pravaah.rbi.org.in).

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-III Role of the Board and Individual Directors

children.title: A. Role of Board of Directors
children.text: A. Role of Board of Directors

children.title: 10. The Board of Directors of an RCB shall be responsible for, inter alia,
children.text:
10. The Board of Directors of an RCB shall be responsible for, inter alia,
(1) formulation of policies;
(2) exercising overall supervision and control over the functioning of the RCB,
leaving day to day administration to the CEO / MD of the RCB.

children.title: 11. An RCB shall ensure that all circulars and other material relating to policies
children.text:
11. An RCB shall ensure that all circulars and other material relating to policies
issued by RBI / NABARD are seen by every member of the Board and also
placed before the Board for suitable action.

children.title: B. Code of conduct
children.text: B. Code of conduct

children.title: 12. An RCB shall lay down a Code of Conduct for its Directors on the Board and
children.text:
12. An RCB shall lay down a Code of Conduct for its Directors on the Board and
Senior Management.

children.title: Explanation: Senior Management shall include top Executives at the level of
children.text:
Explanation: Senior Management shall include top Executives at the level of
General Managers and functional heads.

children.title: 13. The Code of Conduct shall set forth the guiding principles on which an RCB will
children.text:
13. The Code of Conduct shall set forth the guiding principles on which an RCB will
operate and conduct its daily business with its multitudinous stakeholders,
government and regulatory agencies, media, and anyone else with whom it is
connected. It shall recognize that the RCB is a trustee and custodian of public
money and in order to fulfil its fiduciary obligations and responsibilities, it has to
maintain and continue to enjoy the trust and confidence of public at large. The
RCB shall continue to initiate policies which are customer centric, and which
promote financial prudence.

children.title: 14. The Code of Conduct shall be signed by the Directors of an RCB in witness of
children.text:
14. The Code of Conduct shall be signed by the Directors of an RCB in witness of
RCS and NABARD to ensure adherence.

children.title: 15. The directors of an RCB shall adhere to Do's and Don'ts envisaged in the Code
children.text:
15. The directors of an RCB shall adhere to Do's and Don'ts envisaged in the Code
of Conduct as set out below:
(1) Corporate Governance: The Directors of an RCB should:

children.title: (i) be fully aware of the provisions of BR Act, 1949, the bank's byelaws and
children.text:
(i) be fully aware of the provisions of BR Act, 1949, the bank's byelaws and
regulatory prescriptions of RBI/NABARD;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (ii) attend the Board meeting regularly and participate effectively;
children.text: (ii) attend the Board meeting regularly and participate effectively;

children.title: (iii) receive agenda notes in advance and examine as regards to quality of
children.text:
(iii) receive agenda notes in advance and examine as regards to quality of
content and coverage;

children.title: (iv) involve themselves in formulation and adoption of various policies such as
children.text:
(iv) involve themselves in formulation and adoption of various policies such as
credit policy, human resource policy, procurement policy, IT policy,
cybersecurity policy, etc. and monitoring implementation of such policies at
Board meetings;

children.title: (v) ensure adoption of a proper HR policy in relation to recruitment, placement,
children.text:
(v) ensure adoption of a proper HR policy in relation to recruitment, placement,
promotion, transfer and training;

children.title: (vi) be aware of latest developments in banking technology and strive for
children.text:
(vi) be aware of latest developments in banking technology and strive for
adoption of these technologies;

children.title: (vii) review functioning of various Board level / management level Committees
(viii) strengthen internal checks and controls mechanism as regards to
children.text:
(viii) strengthen internal checks and controls mechanism as regards to
segregation of duties and responsibilities and fixing accountability;

children.title: (ix) strengthen internal audit system & vigilance mechanism and its timely
children.text:
(ix) strengthen internal audit system & vigilance mechanism and its timely
reporting and follow up action thereon;

children.title: (x) carefully peruse audit reports & NABARD Inspection Reports and ensure
children.text:
(x) carefully peruse audit reports & NABARD Inspection Reports and ensure
timely action and compliance;

children.title: (xi) undertake review of frauds committed, timely reporting and action taken;
children.text:
(xi) undertake review of frauds committed, timely reporting and action taken;
and

children.title: (xii) ensure observance of statutory requirements and guidelines issued by RBI
children.text:
(xii) ensure observance of statutory requirements and guidelines issued by RBI
/ NABARD.
(2) Business Development: The Directors of an RCB should:

children.title: (i) ensure framing and adoption of Loan policy and manual;
children.text: (i) ensure framing and adoption of Loan policy and manual;

children.title: (ii) review loan portfolio and develop strategies for business diversification for
children.text:
(ii) review loan portfolio and develop strategies for business diversification for
improving the profitability;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (iii) review mobilization of resources and suggest measures for low cost
children.text:
(iii) review mobilization of resources and suggest measures for low cost
resources;

children.title: (iv) ensure efficient management of funds and improving profitability;
children.text: (iv) ensure efficient management of funds and improving profitability;

children.title: (v) review investment management and functioning of Investment Committee;
children.text: (v) review investment management and functioning of Investment Committee;

children.title: (vi) review budgeting of income & expenditure, performance & profitability;
children.text: (vi) review budgeting of income & expenditure, performance & profitability;

children.title: (vii) identify new business activities inclusive of non-fund business;
children.text: (vii) identify new business activities inclusive of non-fund business;

children.title: (viii) review viability of branches with special focus on branches especially loss-
children.text:
(viii) review viability of branches with special focus on branches especially loss-
making branches;

children.title: (ix) ensure loans sanctioned by CEO / GM are put up to the Board;
children.text: (ix) ensure loans sanctioned by CEO / GM are put up to the Board;

children.title: (x) create effective follow up and monitoring system for prompt and timely
children.text:
(x) create effective follow up and monitoring system for prompt and timely
recovery and reduce level of NPAs and overdues;

children.title: (xi) analyse the trends of economy, assist in the discharge of management's
children.text:
(xi) analyse the trends of economy, assist in the discharge of management's
responsibility to public and formulation of measures to improve customer
service and be generally of constructive assistance to the RCB
management; and

children.title: (xii) review arbitration cases.
children.text:
(xii) review arbitration cases.
(3) Development aspects: The Directors should:

children.title: (i) ensure preparation of Development Action Plans for successive financial
children.text:
(i) ensure preparation of Development Action Plans for successive financial
year and monitoring of implementation of plan at quarterly intervals;

children.title: (ii) encourage involvement of the RCB in Govt. sponsored / National priority
(iii) promote and participate in spreading message of financial inclusion and
children.text:
(iii) promote and participate in spreading message of financial inclusion and
digital financial literacy;

children.title: (iv) create environment for effective coordination with higher and lower tiers of
children.text:
(iv) create environment for effective coordination with higher and lower tiers of
the rural co-operative credit structure;

children.title: (v) establish proper MIS and intensifying computerization process;
children.text: (v) establish proper MIS and intensifying computerization process;

children.title: (vi) monitor key risk areas; and
children.text: (vi) monitor key risk areas; and

children.title: (vii) take steps for financial literacy and credit counseling.
children.text: (vii) take steps for financial literacy and credit counseling.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(4) Non-Interference: The directors of an RCB should not:

children.title: (i) interfere in the management functions and involve themselves in routine or
children.text:
(i) interfere in the management functions and involve themselves in routine or
everyday business of the RCB; and

children.title: (ii) send instructions / directions to any individual officer/ employee of the RCB
children.text:
(ii) send instructions / directions to any individual officer/ employee of the RCB
in any manner.
(5) No Sponsorship: The directors of an RCB should not:

children.title: (i) sponsor any loan proposal, buildings and sites for the RCB's premises,
(ii) approach or influence for sanction of any kind of facility;
children.text: (ii) approach or influence for sanction of any kind of facility;

children.title: (iii) participate in Board discussions, if a proposal in which they are directly or
(iv) sponsor any candidate for recruitment or promotion or interfere in the
children.text:
(iv) sponsor any candidate for recruitment or promotion or interfere in the
process of selection / appointment or in transfers of staff;

children.title: (v) do anything which will interfere with an / or be subversive of maintenance
children.text:
(v) do anything which will interfere with an / or be subversive of maintenance
of discipline, good conduct and integrity of the staff;

children.title: (vi) encourage the individual officer/ employee or unions approaching them in
children.text:
(vi) encourage the individual officer/ employee or unions approaching them in
any matter; and

children.title: (vii) conduct RCB's business with a relative or any other person or any firm,
children.text:
(vii) conduct RCB's business with a relative or any other person or any firm,
Company or Association in which the relative or other person is associated
in any significant role.
Provided that, if such a related party transaction is unavoidable, they shall
fully disclose the nature of the related party transaction to the appropriate
authority. Any dealings with a related party must be conducted in such a
way that no preferential treatment is given to that party. In the case of any
other transaction or situation giving rise to conflicts of interests, the
appropriate authority should, after due deliberations, decide on its impact.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(6) Maintain confidentiality: The directors of an RCB should not:

children.title: (i) reveal any information relating to any constituent of the RCB to anyone as
children.text:
(i) reveal any information relating to any constituent of the RCB to anyone as
they are under the oath of secrecy and fidelity. The directors are expected
to ensure confidentiality of the RCB's agenda papers / notes. The board
papers may ordinarily be returned to the RCB after the meeting;

children.title: (ii) call for papers / files / notes recorded by various departments for scrutiny
children.text:
(ii) call for papers / files / notes recorded by various departments for scrutiny
etc. in respect of agenda items to be discussed in the meetings. All
information / clarification that they may require for taking a decision should
be made available by the executive; and

children.title: (iii) display logos of distinctive design of the RCB on their visiting card / letter
children.text:
(iii) display logos of distinctive design of the RCB on their visiting card / letter
head. However, a director may indicate their directorship of the RCB on
their visiting card or letter head.

children.title: Explanation: The Do's and Don'ts are illustrative and not exhaustive and are not to be
children.text:
Explanation: The Do's and Don'ts are illustrative and not exhaustive and are not to be
regarded as a substitute to the specified duties, responsibilities or rights of the Board
of Directors as enunciated in the Cooperative law and / or byelaws of the RCB.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-IV Board Meeting Procedures

children.title: A. Model Agenda of Board Meetings
children.text: A. Model Agenda of Board Meetings

children.title: 16. An illustrative agenda for meeting of the Board of an RCB is suggested below:
Explanation: RCB should suitably modify the same as per their extant
children.text:
Explanation: RCB should suitably modify the same as per their extant

children.title: B. Calendar of Reviews
children.text: B. Calendar of Reviews

children.title: 17. The following matters shall be put up to the Board as per their prescribed
children.text:
17. The following matters shall be put up to the Board as per their prescribed
periodicity:
Calendar of Reviews
Periodicity: Every Board meeting
(1) Funds Management

children.title: (i) Information about funds position of the RCB;
children.text: (i) Information about funds position of the RCB;

children.title: (ii) Compliance with CRR / SLR requirements;
children.text: (ii) Compliance with CRR / SLR requirements;

children.title: (iii) Details of investments in call deposits, non-SLR investments made, securities
children.text:
(iii) Details of investments in call deposits, non-SLR investments made, securities
traded and income earned / loss incurred, if any;

children.title: (iv) Compliance with RBI instructions in regard to maintenance of investments in
children.text:
(iv) Compliance with RBI instructions in regard to maintenance of investments in
Government Securities;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) Details of borrowings availed of from higher financing agencies (StCB /
(vi) Position of deposits mobilized, amount invested in various avenues and
children.text:
(vi) Position of deposits mobilized, amount invested in various avenues and
advances made. The report should not be a fact sheet but should capture
qualitative data on the important market trends, market developments,
regulatory initiatives, etc. between the review periods and should stimulate
constructive suggestions and discussions on critical appraisal of the strategies
followed presently and the need for changes; and

children.title: (vii) Concurrent audit report by internal auditors and compliance report thereon (to
children.text:
(vii) Concurrent audit report by internal auditors and compliance report thereon (to
be placed before the CEO and Chairman every month).
(2) Loans and Advances

children.title: (i) Review of various credit limits sanctioned by Managing Director / Chairman /
children.text:
(i) Review of various credit limits sanctioned by Managing Director / Chairman /
Executive Committee;

children.title: (ii) Sanction of term loans (for setting up / modernization of sugar factory / spinning
children.text:
(ii) Sanction of term loans (for setting up / modernization of sugar factory / spinning
mill / ginning press, etc.) and follow up;

children.title: (iii) Participation in Consortium Finance; and
children.text: (iii) Participation in Consortium Finance; and

children.title: (iv) Adherence to exposure norms prescribed by RBI / NABARD.
children.text:
(iv) Adherence to exposure norms prescribed by RBI / NABARD.
(3) General

children.title: (i) Compliance in respect of outstanding observations of the Board;
children.text: (i) Compliance in respect of outstanding observations of the Board;

children.title: (ii) Review compliance with various Sections of Banking Regulation Act/ RBI Act /
children.text:
(ii) Review compliance with various Sections of Banking Regulation Act/ RBI Act /
Cooperative Societies Act / Rules / byelaws;

children.title: (iii) Circulars received from RBI, NABARD, DICGC, RCS and Apex Bank;
children.text: (iii) Circulars received from RBI, NABARD, DICGC, RCS and Apex Bank;

children.title: (iv) Implementation of the instructions / directives / guidelines issued by RBI /
children.text:
(iv) Implementation of the instructions / directives / guidelines issued by RBI /
NABARD / Govt.; and

children.title: (v) Submission of Statutory and regulatory returns.
children.text:
(v) Submission of Statutory and regulatory returns.
Periodicity: Quarterly
(1) Business Plan: Targets and Achievements

children.title: (i) Performance reviews vis-a-vis business goals such as deposit mobilization,
children.text:
(i) Performance reviews vis-a-vis business goals such as deposit mobilization,
credit disbursal, recovery of loans, etc. The reviews shall:

children.title: (a) be comprehensive;
children.text: (a) be comprehensive;

children.title: (b) cover specifically the RCB's structural / organizational requirement;
children.text: (b) cover specifically the RCB's structural / organizational requirement;

children.title: (c) lead to initiation of business strategies of growth and profitability; and
children.text: (c) lead to initiation of business strategies of growth and profitability; and

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (d) contain RCB's performance under special programmes of credit
children.text:
(d) contain RCB's performance under special programmes of credit
assistance like SHG, etc.

children.title: (ii) Review of high yielding advances sanctioned by the RCB;
children.text: (ii) Review of high yielding advances sanctioned by the RCB;

children.title: (iii) Review of progress in implementation of Govt. sponsored programmes;
children.text: (iii) Review of progress in implementation of Govt. sponsored programmes;

children.title: (iv) A comprehensive report on the recoveries effected under NPA accounts. The
children.text:
(iv) A comprehensive report on the recoveries effected under NPA accounts. The
report shall cover requisite details on the deficiencies observed, systemic
controls required for avoidance of incidence of NPAs, etc. During the review,
the Board shall also look at loan policy caveats on exposure thresholds,
borrower-wise/ industry-wise, etc. and other credit risk management initiatives
on ongoing basis.

children.title: (v) Details of One Time Settlement (OTS) / waiver of irrecoverable loans / interest;
children.text: (v) Details of One Time Settlement (OTS) / waiver of irrecoverable loans / interest;

children.title: (vi) Recovery under legal action (cases of ABN filed, decree / awards obtained and
children.text:
(vi) Recovery under legal action (cases of ABN filed, decree / awards obtained and
EPs filed / executed); and

children.title: (vii) Supervisory reports generated and action taken thereof.
children.text:
(vii) Supervisory reports generated and action taken thereof.
(2) Branch performance

children.title: (i) Branch-wise targets and achievements under various business parameters;
children.text: (i) Branch-wise targets and achievements under various business parameters;

children.title: (ii) Review of branch profitability with suggestions on measures to improve the
(iii) Progress in inspection of branches and important observations of branch
children.text:
(iii) Progress in inspection of branches and important observations of branch
inspections; and

children.title: (iv) Review of discussions held during Branch Manager's review meetings.
children.text:
(iv) Review of discussions held during Branch Manager's review meetings.
(3) Working Results

children.title: (i) An analysis of the working results of the RCB on a quarterly basis along with
children.text:
(i) An analysis of the working results of the RCB on a quarterly basis along with
an analytical note and proposals for future course of action. The RCB's
comparative position shall also be highlighted.
(4) Others

children.title: (i) A comprehensive review of the Vigilance Cell including position of vigilance
(ii) Review of cases of frauds, misappropriations, embezzlements, defalcations,
children.text:
(ii) Review of cases of frauds, misappropriations, embezzlements, defalcations,
etc. together with action for recovery thereof.

children.title: (iii) Review of cases of dacoities and security arrangements in the RCB;

(vii) Observations of the Audit Committee.
Periodicity: Half-yearly

children.title: (i) Half yearly review of investment portfolio (as on 30 September and 31 March);
(ii) A review of operations of the RCB's non-fund business;

children.title: (vi) Review of all aspects relating to the computerization of branches / Regional
children.text:
(vi) Review of all aspects relating to the computerization of branches / Regional
Office / Head Office and also look into Information Technology related needs;

children.title: (vii) Review of cost of funds, yield on deployment of funds, interest rates revision,
children.text:
(vii) Review of cost of funds, yield on deployment of funds, interest rates revision,
etc.;

children.title: (viii) Position of imbalance-society-wise;
children.text: (viii) Position of imbalance-society-wise;

children.text:
(ix) Review compliance of the Fair Practices Code and the functioning of
Grievance Redressal Mechanism at various levels of controlling offices; and

(x) Review of viability of affiliated CCBs / societies. Position of Cadre Fund, share

Periodicity: Yearly

children.title: (i) Working results - Comparison of various parameters with working results of
children.text:
(i) Working results - Comparison of various parameters with working results of
other RCBs and should include analysis of Balance Sheet prescribed by RBI;

children.title: (ii) Audit Report - Long Form Audit Report (LFAR) of the RCB along with RCB's
children.text:
(ii) Audit Report - Long Form Audit Report (LFAR) of the RCB along with RCB's
comments on the auditors' observations;

children.title: <image>

children.title: (iii) Review of all policies - An annual review of all policies formulated by the RCB
(iv) Corporate Budget Review of budget proposals made for various expenditure
children.text:
(iv) Corporate Budget Review of budget proposals made for various expenditure
vis-a-vis actual expenditure and reasons for large scale deviations, if any;

children.title: (v) Review of working of Head Office Departments;
children.text: (v) Review of working of Head Office Departments;

children.text:
(vi) Statutory inspection report and compliance report thereon (to be submitted to
NABARD);

children.title: (vii) Review of findings of inspection conducted by StCB and action taken thereon;

children.text:
(x) Note on Man-power Planning and Development and periodical review in
respect thereof, as well as policy relating to recruitment / promotion / transfer /
staff welfare.

children.title: 18. An RCB shall submit a quarterly return to concerned Regional Office of NABARD
children.text:
18. An RCB shall submit a quarterly return to concerned Regional Office of NABARD
(as per the format in the Annex) by the 10th of April / July / October / January in
respect of the status of submission of above agenda notes to the Board.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-V Committees of the Board

children.title: A. Audit Committee of Board
children.text: A. Audit Committee of Board

children.title: 19. In order to ensure and enhance the quality and effectiveness of internal audit /
children.text:
19. In order to ensure and enhance the quality and effectiveness of internal audit /
inspection as a management tool, an RCB shall set up an independent Audit
Committee at the Board level for overseeing and providing direction to the
internal audit / inspection machinery / Statutory (external) Auditors and other
executives of the RCB.

children.title: 20. Constitution of the Committee - The Board of Directors of an RCB shall ensure
children.text:
20. Constitution of the Committee - The Board of Directors of an RCB shall ensure
that members of its Audit Committee are independent, understand banking and
financial matters and have the capability to serve as effective Audit Committee
members, including having the ability to formulate and ask probing questions
about the RCB's financial reporting process and to challenge management
decisions, if and when necessary. While constituting the Committee, an RCB
shall ensure that:
(1) the Committee comprises three or four directors only, one of whom shall act
as the Chairman of the Committee;
(2) the Chairman and the CEO / MD of the RCB is not a member of the Audit
Committee; and
(3) one qualified locally available Chartered Accountant shall be co-opted.
Provided that, if the elected Board of an RCB has been superseded, the
Administrator shall appoint two or three members having expertise in banking,
accountancy, finance, etc. for constitution of this Committee and such an Audit
Committee shall have the Chartered Accountant as its Chairman.

children.title: 21. The Audit Committee shall be given direct, unfettered, and independent access
children.text:
21. The Audit Committee shall be given direct, unfettered, and independent access
to management, internal audit, and statutory auditors. The Committee shall be
able to communicate in confidence with these three groups independently of
each other.

children.title: 22. Role of the Committee - The ACB of an RCB shall assist the Board of Directors
children.text:
22. Role of the Committee - The ACB of an RCB shall assist the Board of Directors
in fulfilling its oversight responsibilities by reviewing:

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(1) the financial reports and other financial information provided by the RCB to
any statutory body or the public;
(2) the RCB's systems of internal controls regarding funds, accounting, legal
compliance, ethics that management and the Board have established; and,
(3) the RCB's auditing, accounting and financial reporting processes.

children.title: 23. Accordingly, the Committee shall:
children.text:
23. Accordingly, the Committee shall:
(1) serve as a proactive, effective and objective mechanism to monitor the
RCB's financial reporting process and internal control systems;
(2) review and appraise the audit efforts of the RCB's external auditors and
internal audit department;
(3) review and approve the compliances furnished by the RCB in respect of
audit reports and inspection reports issued by NABARD / RCB's internal
audit department; and
(4) provide an open channel of communication among the external /
departmental auditors, senior management, the internal audit department
and the Board of Directors.

children.title: 24. Responsibilities of the Committee - The suggested Agenda for the Audit
children.text:
24. Responsibilities of the Committee - The suggested Agenda for the Audit
Committee is indicated below:
(1) Review:

children.title: (i) Recommend to the full Board for shareholder's approval, the appointment,
children.text:
(i) Recommend to the full Board for shareholder's approval, the appointment,
reappointment, removal, or replacement and terms of appointment of
auditors, and the fee payable to them for the audit, taking into consideration
any relationship between the auditors and the RCB that may impact the
independence of the auditors in carrying out the audit.

children.title: (ii) Approve the terms of engagement of the services of the external / statutory
children.text:
(ii) Approve the terms of engagement of the services of the external / statutory
auditors for rendering any other professional services to the RCB and the
fee therefor.

children.title: (iii) Review and examine with the management, the annual financial statements
children.text:
(iii) Review and examine with the management, the annual financial statements
and auditor's report thereon before submission to the Board for approval,
with particular reference to:

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (a) Any changes in accounting policies and practices;
children.text: (a) Any changes in accounting policies and practices;

children.title: (b) Major accounting entries involving estimates based on the
children.text:
(b) Major accounting entries involving estimates based on the
exercise of judgement by management;

children.title: (c) Significant adjustments made in the financial statements arising
children.text:
(c) Significant adjustments made in the financial statements arising
out of audit findings;

children.title: (d) Compliance with the applicable accounting standards;
children.text: (d) Compliance with the applicable accounting standards;

children.title: (e) Compliance with listing and other legal requirements relating to
(f) Auditor's statement on internal control systems prior to
children.text:
(f) Auditor's statement on internal control systems prior to
endorsement by the Board;

children.title: (g) The going concern assumption;
children.text: (g) The going concern assumption;

children.title: (h) Disclosure of any related-party transaction, i.e., transactions of
children.text:
(h) Disclosure of any related-party transaction, i.e., transactions of
the RCB with its Directors, senior executives, their relatives, or
associated concerns, that may potentially conflict with the RCB's
interests at large;

children.title: (i) Modified opinion(s) in the draft audit report; and
children.text: (i) Modified opinion(s) in the draft audit report; and

children.title: (j) Consider suggesting adoption of Accounting Standards on
children.text:
(j) Consider suggesting adoption of Accounting Standards on
voluntary basis in tune with industry best practices.

children.title: (iv) Review of IS audit observations and compliance.
children.text: (iv) Review of IS audit observations and compliance.

children.title: (v) Review follow-up action on the Internal Inspection / Internal Audit Reports,
children.text:
(v) Review follow-up action on the Internal Inspection / Internal Audit Reports,
particularly of "unsatisfactory" branches, and large branches and also on
concurrent audit observations.

children.title: (vi) Review RCB's compliance with statutory provisions of the Banking
children.text:
(vi) Review RCB's compliance with statutory provisions of the Banking
Regulation Act 1949 (AACS), with particular reference to Section 18, 24,
29, 31, 35A etc./ Section 42 of the RBI Act, 1934 in case of Scheduled
RCBs.

children.title: (vii) Review the information security audit mechanism in the RCB with particular
children.text:
(vii) Review the information security audit mechanism in the RCB with particular
reference to Disaster Recovery System and Business Continuity Planning.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (viii) Periodically review the progress in reconciliation of entries in Inter-bank and
children.text:
(viii) Periodically review the progress in reconciliation of entries in Inter-bank and
Inter-branch accounts and reconciliation of RCB accounts.

children.title: (ix) Review of frauds and action taken to recover the amount involved, to avoid
children.text:
(ix) Review of frauds and action taken to recover the amount involved, to avoid
recurrence of such frauds in future.

children.title: (x) Review any significant disagreement among management and the
children.text:
(x) Review any significant disagreement among management and the
accounts department or the internal audit department in connection with the
preparation of the financial statements.

children.title: (xi) Review, with the management, the quarterly financial statements before
children.text:
(xi) Review, with the management, the quarterly financial statements before
submission to the Board for approval.

children.title: (xii) Review and exercise oversight over the functioning of the Whistle Blower
children.text:
(xii) Review and exercise oversight over the functioning of the Whistle Blower
Mechanism.

children.title: (xiii) Ensure that Senior Management is taking necessary corrective actions to
children.text:
(xiii) Ensure that Senior Management is taking necessary corrective actions to
address the findings and recommendations of internal auditors and external
auditors in a timely manner.
(2) Policy:

children.title: (i) Fix accountability for unsatisfactory compliance of inspection / audit reports,
children.text:
(i) Fix accountability for unsatisfactory compliance of inspection / audit reports,
delay in compliance and non-rectification of deficiencies.

children.title: (ii) Ensure the RCB's adherence to KYC & PMLA guidelines issued by the RBI
children.text:
(ii) Ensure the RCB's adherence to KYC & PMLA guidelines issued by the RBI
/ NABARD and if any violations are observed, action taken thereon to avoid
recurrence of the same.

children.title: (iii) Recommend to the Board of Directors the selection of the Internal Auditor
children.text:
(iii) Recommend to the Board of Directors the selection of the Internal Auditor
considering their independence and effectiveness.

children.title: (iv) Periodical review of the accounting policies, systems and controls in the
children.text:
(iv) Periodical review of the accounting policies, systems and controls in the
RCB with a view to ensuring greater transparency in the RCB's accounts
and adequacy of accounting controls to address the risks faced or likely to
be faced by the RCB.

children.title: (v) Consider and approve, if appropriate, major changes to the RCB's auditing
children.text:
(v) Consider and approve, if appropriate, major changes to the RCB's auditing
principles and practices as suggested by the accounts department,
management, or the internal audit Department / External Auditors /
NABARD Inspection Team.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (vi) Establish, review and update periodically a Code of Ethical Conduct and
children.text:
(vi) Establish, review and update periodically a Code of Ethical Conduct and
ensure that management has established a system to enforce this Code.
(3) Compliance:

children.title: (i) Peruse the latest Inspection Report of NABARD and status of compliance
children.text:
(i) Peruse the latest Inspection Report of NABARD and status of compliance
therewith.

children.title: (ii) Compliance to Statutory Audit Report.
children.text: (ii) Compliance to Statutory Audit Report.

children.title: (iii) Divergence is observed and pointed out in the Inspection Reports which
children.text:
(iii) Divergence is observed and pointed out in the Inspection Reports which
are sent to the RCB. To see that the RCB / Statutory Auditors have been
ensuring proper classification of such assets while finalizing the books of
accounts as at the end of next financial year.

children.title: (iv) Review the integrity of the RCB's financial reporting processes, both
children.text:
(iv) Review the integrity of the RCB's financial reporting processes, both
internal and external, especially, the correctness of NPAs and provisioning
for impaired assets.

children.title: (v) Review omission on the part of internal inspecting officials / concurrent
children.text:
(v) Review omission on the part of internal inspecting officials / concurrent
auditors to detect serious irregularities, which come to light later.

children.title: (vi) Certification of physical possession of securities as reported to RBI &
children.text:
(vi) Certification of physical possession of securities as reported to RBI &
NABARD every quarter by Internal / Departmental Auditors or concurrent
auditors.

children.title: (vii) Perform any other activities consistent with the guidelines of RBI /
children.text:
(vii) Perform any other activities consistent with the guidelines of RBI /
NABARD, the RCB's byelaws and Co-operative Societies' Act, as the
Committee or the Board deems necessary or appropriate.

children.title: (viii) Review reasons for defaults in payment to the depositors and creditors in
children.text:
(viii) Review reasons for defaults in payment to the depositors and creditors in
case of such defaults.

children.title: 25. Meetings of the Committee - The audit committee shall meet at least once in a
children.text:
25. Meetings of the Committee - The audit committee shall meet at least once in a
quarter. Besides, the Committee should meet at least half-yearly with
management, the chief of the statutory auditing / inspection team and the
department of accounts in separate sessions to discuss any matters that the
Committee or each of these groups believe should be discussed privately.

children.title: 26. Calendar of Reviews - The following reviews shall be put up to the Audit
children.text:
26. Calendar of Reviews - The following reviews shall be put up to the Audit
Committees of an RCB at their corresponding periodicity:

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(1) Quarterly:

children.title: (i) Inter-branch reconciliation;
children.text: (i) Inter-branch reconciliation;

children.title: (ii) Performance of Concurrent Audit / Inspection Department;
children.text: (ii) Performance of Concurrent Audit / Inspection Department;

children.title: (iii) Inspection reports of poorly rated branches - Progress in rectification of
children.text:
(iii) Inspection reports of poorly rated branches - Progress in rectification of
deficiencies;

children.title: (iv) Review of arrears in balancing of books, clearing differences, sundry /
children.text:
(iv) Review of arrears in balancing of books, clearing differences, sundry /
suspense items; and

children.title: (v) IS audit compliances.
children.text:
(v) IS audit compliances.
(2) Yearly:

children.title: (i) Compliance with observations made by the statutory auditor; and
children.text: (i) Compliance with observations made by the statutory auditor; and

children.title: (ii) Compliance with observations made in NABARD's Inspection Report.
children.text: (ii) Compliance with observations made in NABARD's Inspection Report.

children.title: B. Risk Management Committee
children.text: B. Risk Management Committee

children.title: 27. Risks are usually defined by the adverse impact of several distinct sources of
children.text:
27. Risks are usually defined by the adverse impact of several distinct sources of
uncertainty on profitability. The principal banking risks are credit risk, liquidity risk,
interest rate risk, market risk, foreign exchange risk and solvency risk. Risk
management covers all risk information systems, reporting and subsequent
actions.

children.title: 28. In order to improve the ability to identify, measure, monitor and control the overall
children.text:
28. In order to improve the ability to identify, measure, monitor and control the overall
level of risks it is exposed to, an RCB shall constitute a Risk Management
Committee as a Sub-Committee of the Board.

children.title: 29. Constitution of the Committee - The following instructions shall apply to the
children.text:
29. Constitution of the Committee - The following instructions shall apply to the
constitution of a Risk Management Committee by an RCB:
(1) the Committee shall include the CEO and Heads of Credit, Investment,
Operational Risk departments, etc.;
(2) the Head of IT Department shall be a special invitee to the meetings; and
(3) there may be sub-committees and support committees at the RCB's
discretion.

children.title: 30. Role of the Committee - The Committee shall:
children.text: 30. Role of the Committee - The Committee shall:

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
(1) ensure a proper Loan Review Mechanism is put in place for constantly
evaluating the quality of credit portfolio and to bring out qualitative
improvements in credit administration;
(2) monitor the RCB's handling of concentration of risk and may require that
the Credit Department reports to them any such exposure exceeding a
specified limit or exposures to large borrowers;
(3) ensure an effective liquidity management policy which spells out funding
strategies, liquidity planning under alternative scenarios, prudential limits,
liquidity reporting / reviewing, etc.; and
(4) establish well defined internal control systems which include segregation of
duties, clear management reporting lines and adequate operating
procedures and compliance thereof.

children.title: 31. Responsibilities of the Committee - The suggested Agenda for the Risk
children.text:
31. Responsibilities of the Committee - The suggested Agenda for the Risk
Management Committee is indicated below:
(1) approve the policy, strategy and methods for risk management, by
evaluating the overall risks faced by the RCB and determining the
acceptable level of risks, its management and control in the best interest of
the RCB; and
(2) oversee all risk management functions in the RCB by undertaking the
following functions:

children.title: (i) approve the policies and strategies for implementing integrated risk
children.text:
(i) approve the policies and strategies for implementing integrated risk
management system, for addressing various risks faced by the RCB;

children.title: (ii) oversee functions of Credit Risk Management Committee (CRMC),
children.text:
(ii) oversee functions of Credit Risk Management Committee (CRMC),
Asset Liability Management Committee (ALCO), Operational Risk
Management Committee (ORMC) and Information Security
Committee (ISC);

children.title: (iii) set policies and guidelines for measurement, management and
children.text:
(iii) set policies and guidelines for measurement, management and
reporting of credit risk, market risk and operational risk;

children.title: (iv) set risk mitigation and stop-loss parameters in respect of all the three
children.text:
(iv) set risk mitigation and stop-loss parameters in respect of all the three
risks;

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: (v) ensure Management Processes (including people, systems,
(vi) ensure robustness and effectiveness of financial / rating models and
children.text:
(vi) ensure robustness and effectiveness of financial / rating models and
their appraisal systems for assessing various risks faced by the RCB;

children.title: (vii) review and approve the Internal Capital adequacy Assessment
children.text:
(vii) review and approve the Internal Capital adequacy Assessment
Process;

children.title: (viii) approval / review of credit rating process, fixation of exposure
children.text:
(viii) approval / review of credit rating process, fixation of exposure
ceilings for various types of exposures, geographies, sectors,
industries etc., and based on internal / external rating, borrower
category / groups etc.;

children.title: (ix) ensure availability of qualified and competent officers and risk
(x) decide / approve adoption of technology / appropriate and adequate
children.text:
(x) decide / approve adoption of technology / appropriate and adequate
MIS system needed for risk management;

children.title: (xi) reinforce the culture and awareness of risk management throughout
children.text:
(xi) reinforce the culture and awareness of risk management throughout
the organization that would attach high priority on effective risk
management and adherence to sound internal controls; and

children.title: (xii) assess feasibility of shift to Risk Based Internal Audit (RBIA) and
children.text:
(xii) assess feasibility of shift to Risk Based Internal Audit (RBIA) and
prepare a road map for transition.

children.title: <image>
children.text:
<image>
Chapter-VI Repeal and other provisions

children.title: A. Repeal and saving
children.text: A. Repeal and saving

children.title: 32. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
children.text:
32. With the issue of these Directions, the existing Directions, instructions, and
guidelines relating to governance as applicable to Rural Co-operative Banks
stand repealed, as communicated vide circular DOR.RRC.REC.302/33-01-
010/2025-26 dated November 28, 2025. The Directions, instructions, and
guidelines repealed prior to the issuance of these Directions shall continue to
remain repealed.

children.title: 33. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
children.text:
33. Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof. All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed
Directions/instructions/guidelines shall be deemed as being governed by these
Directions. Further, the repeal of these directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
not in any way prejudicially affect:
(1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder;
(2) any, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment incurred in respect of any
contravention committed thereunder;
(3) any investigation, legal proceeding, or remedy in respect of any such right,
privilege, obligation, liability, penalty, forfeiture, or punishment as aforesaid;
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may be instituted,
continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may
be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guidelines had not been
repealed.

children.title: B. Application of other laws not barred
children.text: B. Application of other laws not barred

children.title: 34. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
children.text:
34. The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation
of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.

children.title: <image>
children.text: <image>

children.title: C. Interpretations
children.text: C. Interpretations

children.title: 35. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
children.text:
35. For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the RBI may, if it considers necessary, issue necessary
clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the interpretation of any
provision of these Directions given by the RBI shall be final and binding.
(Scenta Joy) Chief General Manager

children.text: <image>

children.text:
